quests.Title: \i[314]Flower Picking
Description: A young child in Verdin Village by the name of \c[4]Flora\c[0] asked me to pick a few flowers for her. Nothing crazy, just a \ii[3]\c[0], \ii[4]\c[0], and \ii[5]\c[0].
I \c[4]delivered the flowers\c[0] to \c[4]Flora\c[0]! She was ecstatic, and bestowed me with \ia[242]\c[0]!

quests.Title: \i[258]The Headman's Delivery
Description: I have to go to the \c[4]general store\c[0] and pick up a delivery for the \c[4]Village Headman\c[0]. I've been given 10B to get the package. \c[4]The general store should be in the bottom right of Solus Town\c[0], according to the Village Headman.
The \c[4]package\c[0] has been obtained! Now I have to run back to the \c[4]Village Headman\c[0] and give it to him.
After giving the \ii[6] to the \c[4]Village Headman\c[0], he thanked me. I wonder what's inside... anyways, the Village Headman told me to prepare for training.

quests.Title: \i[240]Crate and Seek
Description: My final task from the Village Headman is to find the \i[240]\c[4]Old Key\c[0]. It shouldn't be too hard... right? It's on a crate in the first floor of his house.
Now that I've found the \i[240]\c[4]Old Key\c[0], it's time to return to the Village Headman.
After I found the \i[240]\c[4]Old Key\c[0] for the \c[4]Village Headman\c[0], he instructed me to meet him in his bedroom, the room where this all started... what could he want?
Subtext: Task 3/3. Why is he giving you busy work, this is a bit pointless...

quests.Title: \i[1]Defeat \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0]
Description:
Maybe it's just rumors... but the potential threat of a \c[18]demon king\c[0] seeking to \c[4]destroy the human race\c[0] is too great to ignore. Besides, it finally gives me a purpose.\n

\n\c[0]For now, it's just me and \N[2]. I'm sure that as our journey goes on, we'll meet \c[4]new party members\c[0], get some \c[4]better gear\c[0], and hopefully find out more about \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0]... if \c[18]he\c[0]'s even real.

It's been a while since I flipped to this entry...\n

\n\c[0]We found and fought \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0], but \c[18]he\c[0] interrupted the fight and \c[4]escaped through a portal in a strange place\c[0]. I want to write more, but \N[3] is rushing us to \c[4]find the king\c[0] so we can yell at him. I'll write more after \c[4]we figure out what the next plan of action is\c[0].

We found and fought \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0], but \c[18]he\c[0] interrupted the fight and \c[4]escaped through a portal in a strange place\c[0]. \c[4]True Telluria Castle\c[0] was meant to keep \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0] sealed inside, but we \c[4]let him escape\c[0]... and a result, the \c[4]Great Collapse\c[0] is \c[4]beginning once more\c[0]!

\c[0]The \c[4]king\c[0] and \c[4]Oriel\c[0] are understandably worried. With \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0] now free to do as \c[18]he\c[0] pleases, it's going to be a lot harder to defeat \c[18]him\c[0]. That first fight was hard enough, and \c[18]he\c[0] wasn't even at \c[18]his\c[0] \c[4]full power\c[0]...

\c[4]Oriel\c[0] cast a spell to locate \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0]; she believes he has attacked \c[4]Greenfield\c[0]. We need to get to \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0] and put an end to his reign of terror once and for all!

From saving the survivors of \c[4]Greenfield\c[0] to infiltrating the \c[4]Order of the \c[30]Purple\c[0], we've done a lot since \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0] escaped. After all that, we finally have a solid lead on \c[18]him\c[0].

\N[3] figured out that the \c[4]Order of the \c[30]Purple\c[0] plans to \c[4]resurrect\c[0] the \c[4]corpse of the fallen god\c[0]! Their ritual of possessing the statue with a fragment of the \c[30]Purple\c[0] was just practice for the real thing. The corpse is in the \c[4]$WASTELAND\c[0], a barren region in the \c[4]southwestern part of Telluria\c[0].

\c[0]What threats lie ahead? The last few fights have pretty intense... I can't imagine what \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0] has in store for us.
Objectives List: Speak with the \c[4]Village Headman\c[0] in the \c[4]church\c[0]
Attend the meeting in the \c[4]king's throne room\c[0]
Speak with the \c[4]king of Telluria\c[0] again
After preparing, meet the \c[4]king of Telluria\c[4] in the \c[4]throne room\c[0]
Find and defeat \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0] in \c[4]True Telluria Castle\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[529]Into Verdin
Description: After the \c[4]Village Headman's talk\c[0], he told me to keep the Squire gear, gave me a few bits and a map, and sent me \c[4]off to Verdin Village\c[0]. According to the map, it's a short trip, so there shouldn't be anything too difficult, thankfully. The Forest Arena is the only thing in the way, and I already defeated the Mini Green Slime that was in there.

I \c[4]arrived in Verdin Village\c[0] and met \c[4]\N[2]\c[0]. He's definitely a bit over-enthusiastic about everything, but from what I've seen of him so far, he's a hard worker and a pretty nice guy. Me and the guard that accompanied me helped \N[2] defeat \c[4]the Banditos\c[0], and he gave me a small tour of the village in return. Now he's got \c[4]a couple more chores for me\c[0]...

quests.Title: \i[126]A Rare Find
Description: The \c[4]Village Headman\c[0] told me to bring him \c[4]a certain rare item\c[0]. He said that \c[4]I'll know what it is once I see it\c[0]. I have... not the foggiest clue what it could be. I assume I won't be finding it for a while.

\c[4]I showed the Village Headman the power (\ia[30]) we took from Laeryidyean\c[0]. He says that \c[4]this was just what he was looking for\c[0]! I didn't expect it to be this, or anything remotely close to this, but it's pretty cool.
\c[4]I spared Laeryidyean\c[0], but the \c[4]Village Headman\c[0] wasn't aware of this. He told me to \c[4]make sure I take the power next time I defeat a boss monster\c[0].

quests.Title: \i[150]Bandito Control
Description: After \c[4]\N[2]\c[4] and I worked together along with the guard to \c[4]defeat Big Mouth\c[0], we picked up the \ii[13] that was stolen from the \c[4]general store\c[0]. \N[2] said it'd be a good idea to \c[4]return it ASAP\c[0].
I returned the \ii[13] to the \c[4]Verdin Village general store\c[0]. I was thanked and given \c[4]\i[262]10 bits\c[0].

quests.Title: \i[5]Scarecrow Straightening
Description: \c[4]Straightening scarecrows\c[0]. This is what I'm doing with my time? I guess I can't pass up a free bed for the night...
Well, the \c[4]scarecrows have been straightened\c[0]. That was wholly unremarkable.
Objectives List: Straighten the scarecrow in the west field
Straighten the scarecrow in the east field
Straighten the scarecrow in the field outside of town
Rewards List: Free bed at the \c[4]Verdin Village Inn\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[196]A Sticky Situation
Description: According to this \N[2] guy, if I want to keep \c[4]exploring further south\c[0], I have to find \c[4]Green Slime Cavern\c[0], fight some \c[4]Green Slimes\c[0], and \c[4]make it to the other end\c[0]. Hopefully it's not too bad...
I successfully made it through \c[4]Green Slime Cavern\c[0]! That means I can \c[4]continue further down Adon\c[0]!
Objectives List: Find the entrance to \c[4]Green Slime Cavern\c[0] at the north side of Verdin Village

quests.Title: \i[147]A Traveler's Sword
Description: \c[4]Adam\c[0] needs a basic weapon for his own journey. \c[4]A \iw[5] should suffice\c[0], according to him. The \c[4]general store\c[0] should offer it, if I recall correctly.
I got \c[4]Adam\c[0] the \c[4]\iw[5]\c[0]! He was now able to set off on his journey. \c[4]Perhaps we will meet each other once more\c[0] during our adventures down Adon.

quests.Title: \i[231]Sector By Sector
Description: We have one goal: \c[4]reach Haven Harbor\c[0]. Sound simple, right? But \c[4]just because it's simple doesn't mean it's going to be easy\c[0]. We're going to have to stay alert, who knows what will try to stop us later on?

At long last, \c[4]we've reached Haven Harbor\c[0]! It's been an arduous journey down \c[4]Adon\c[0], but now we can kick our feet back and relax.\n

\n\c[0]Well... not really. \c[4]Matt\c[0] and the \c[4]Village Elder\c[0] really want us to investigate the \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0] \c[4]rumors\c[0]. And honestly, I've gotten a lot more curious. Is \c[18]he\c[0] really real? I guess we'll find out.
\c[4]We defeated Laeryidyean and spared him\c[0]. Now, \c[4]onto Telluria\c[0]!

\c[4]We killed Laeryidyean\c[0].

\c[0]I know that this should feel triumphant, as we've just lifted a continent-wide curse on Adon, but... I don't know, it just doesn't feel right. Something feels off about all of this. Laeryidyean was a horrible monster and had to be defeated, and I don't regret landing that final blow, but those words he spoke on the brink of his death... that was downright unsettling, as \N[3] pointed out.

\c[0]Well, \c[4]onto Telluria\c[0], I suppose. Worries only keep you up at night.

quests.Title: \i[797]Saving The Forest: Part I
Description: \c[4]Mark\c[0] has instructed us to defeat \c[4]some Blue Slimes\c[0] at \c[4]the south of the Forest Clearing\c[0]. There's only three of them, thankfully, so as long as we've got the proper techniques down, it shouldn't be too hard.

\c[4]We defeated the Blue Slimes\c[0]! \c[4]Mark\c[0] immediately instructed us to \c[4]head to the west where the Dark Forest is\c[0]. What lies there? Probably just more fighting, honestly... but a puzzle or two would never hurt.

quests.Title: \i[788]Saving The Forest: Part II
Description: \c[4]Mark\c[0] has another task for us: \c[4]slay the monsters in the Dark Forest\c[0]. They've taken over the place, and they need to be stopped! There are quite a few, though...

\c[4]Mark\c[0] and \c[4]Milky\c[0] were very happy that we defeated all those monsters in the \c[4]Dark Forest\c[0]. They wished us farewell as we headed down to Sector III.\n

\n\c[0]I liked the two of them, I hope we get to see them again sometime. But now... what could be ahead?
Objectives List: Fight your way through the \c[4]Dark Forest\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[457]A Traveler's Trial
Description: \c[4]Adam\c[0] told us that he's going to to head to the \c[4]north of the Dark Forest\c[0] for a \c[4]slight challenge\c[0]. Maybe if we follow him \c[4]he'll give us some extra loot\c[0]? Could be worth a shot.

We followed \c[4]Adam\c[0] and reached the \c[4]north side of the Dark Forest\c[0]. There, he decided to give us a \c[4]\ia[12]\c[0] he found in the chest!\n

\n\c[0]That was pretty cool of him, \c[4]hopefully we will meet again later\c[0]. Pretty nice guy.
Objectives List: Follow \c[4]Adam\c[0] through the \c[4]north of the Dark Forest\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[416]A Traveler's Deal
Description: We need to\c[4]collect 80 \i[262]Bits\c[0] and buy \c[4]Adam\c[0]'s \c[4]\iw[11]\c[0]. Seems like an okay deal...

\c[4]We bought the \iw[11] from Adam\c[0]. He was very happy that we bought it, and \c[4]he set out to head further down Adon\c[0].\n

\n\c[0]Good things always happen when we cross paths... I wouldn't complain if we saw him again going down Adon.

quests.Title: \i[261]A Traveler's Memento
Description:
\c[4]Adam\c[0] needs us to complete a simple task: \c[4]head inside the Bandito Hideout\c[0], get to one of the \c[4]guest rooms\c[0], and find some \ii[13].\n

\n\c[0]I don't know about this... the Banditos probably wouldn't like see rummaging around in their drawers as us just trying to make peace. Also... who keeps raid loot sitting around in their drawers? Seems like a strange place to put it.
\c[4]We gave Adam the \ii[13]\c[0]. He thanked us and gifted us a \ii[23]\c[0]. What could he be doing with that \ii[13]\c[0], though? Well, no matter, we're both better off now.

quests.Title: \i[450]Bandito Control... Again
Description: This is it. Our final showdown with Four-Toed Kevin is just ahead. \c[4]Let's go and defeat Four-Toed Kevin\c[0]!

We defeated \c[4]Four-Toed Kevin\c[0] fair and square! After some talking, he's decided to \c[4]stay on the straight and narrow from now on\c[0]. Isn't this great to turn a foe to a friend?\n

\n\c[0]But on that note... where are \c[4]Big Mouth\c[0] and \c[4]Smart Mouth\c[0]? I hope they won't cause any trouble down the line...

quests.Title: \i[262]What Goes Around
Description: The \c[4]distressed sailor\c[0] needs us to get... \c[4]1500 bits\c[0]! Wow! That's... a lot of money. However, there's no other way to get a boat... so I guess we have to start scrounging up some money.

\N[2]\c[0], \c[4]Matt\c[0], the \c[4]Village Elder\c[0], and \c[4]I\c[0] talked to the \c[4]distressed sailor\c[0] and helped him find some alternate methods to get money. Luckily, \c[4]he still gave us his old boat\c[0]! We can get down Adon now!\n

\n\c[0]That means... \c[4]Laeryidyean\c[0] is just ahead. I know I'm supposed to be the champion of Solus Town and leader of the party meant to defeat \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0]... yet I can't help but feel nervous.

quests.Title: \i[99]Watership Down
Description:
The \c[4]mayor of Greenfield\c[0] is in need of our help! All the soldiers that he's sent on errands have been terrorized by a \c[4]Water Elemental\c[0], and he needs us to stop her.\n

\n\c[0]The Mayor is an... strange guy, but we should probably lend him some help. I'm sure we can get some info about \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0] in return.
The \c[4]Greenfield mayor\c[0] was very thankful that we defeated the \c[4]Water Elemental\c[0]. We'll ask if he has any \c[4]intel\c[0] once we get back to \c[4]his office\c[0].
Nice, we defeated the \c[4]Water Elemental\c[0]! We should check out \c[4]Greenfield\c[0] and see if we can claim some sort of reward... maybe \c[4]intel\c[0] from their \c[4]leader\c[0]?
The \c[4]Mayor of Greenfield\c[0] unfortunately didn't know anything about \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0]... oh well, at least we got some \c[4]loot\c[0] from the Water Elemental\c[0].
The \c[4]Mayor of Greenfield\c[0] unfortunately didn't know anything about \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0]... oh well, at least we got some \c[4]loot\c[0] from the Water Elemental\c[0].
Objectives List: Meet the Mayor in the \c[4]Lakeside Clearing\c[0]
Speak with the \c[4]mayor of Greenfield\c[0] in \c[4]his office\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[523]Vow to Serve
Description:
\c[4]Time to get political!\c[0] When we tried to cross from the \c[4]Blazing Sands\c[0] to \c[4]Telluria Field\c[0], we were stopped at the \c[4]Northern Blazing Garrison\c[0], since we're \c[4]nobodies\c[0] in the eyes of the \c[4]empire\c[0].\n

\n\c[0]That's when we met \c[4]Baron Smirnoff\c[0], the guy in charge of the \c[4]garrison\c[0]. He said he couldn't let us through, but if I \c[4]got endorsed by the rulers of the 3 cities of Southern Telluria\c[0], he could promote me to a \c[4]knight\c[0], granting us \c[4]passage through any Tellurian blockade\c[0]!

What a finale to our journey through \c[4]Southern Telluria\c[0]! We \c[4]appeased the leaders\c[0] of \c[4]Greenfield\c[0], \c[4]Solus Valley\c[0], and \c[4]Bladesville\c[0], making me worthy enough in the eyes of \c[4]Baron Smirnoff\c[0] to be made an official \c[4]Tellurian knight\c[0]. Little did I expect we'd face \c[4]Baron Smirnoff\c[0] in an \c[4]epic showdown\c[0], rather than a typical ceremony!\n

\n\c[0]Now that I'm officially a \c[4]knight of Telluria\c[0], there's nothing stopping us from crossing through the \c[4]Northern Desert Garrison\c[0] and into \c[4]Telluria Field\c[0]. The journey continues...
Objectives List: Get endorsed by the \c[4]Village Chief\c[0] of \c[4]Solus Valley\c[0]
Get endorsed by the \c[4]Town Master\c[0] of \c[4]Bladesville\c[0]
Be \c[4]prepared\c[0] in the \c[4]Northern Desert Garrison\c[0] at \c[4]8:00\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[668]The Ghastly Giant Goblin!
Description: The \c[4]Village Chief\c[0] of \c[4]Solus Valley\c[0] informed of us the \c[4]Giant Goblin\c[0] terrorising their fishermen. He offered to \c[4]pay us\c[0], so we could probably see if he knows anything about \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0]!
We successfully defeated the \c[4]Giant Goblin\c[0]! Let's go talk to the \c[4]Village Chief\c[0] and see what he's got for us.

After informing the \c[4]Village Chief\c[0] that we defeated the \c[4]Giant Goblin\c[0], we inquired about \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0].

\c[0]He actually HAS heard about \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0]... kind of. Nothing recent, but when he was a \c[4]child\c[0], he was told cautionary tales of the \c[18]Lord of Shadow\c[0], a figure so evil \c[18]he\c[0] would decapitate and eat the heads of misbehaving children.

\c[0]Sounds like something your parents would make up to get you to do chores...
Objectives List: Speak to the \c[4]Village Chief\c[0] in his residence

quests.Title: \i[331]Under Construction
Description: \c[4]Jeremy\c[0] asked me to pitch in some items for \c[4]finishing repairs of the Solus Town tavern\c[0]. They can be acquired by \c[4]fighting enemies\c[0], \c[4]picking them up off the ground\c[0], and \c[4]cooking\c[0]... I'll have to keep an eye out.
\c[4]We gave the materials to Jeremy\c[0]! He thanked us for our help, afterwards giving us \c[4]another quest\c[0].

quests.Title: \i[215]Local Brewery
Description: \c[4]Jeremy\c[0] asked us to find ingredients to test new \c[4]potion recipes\c[0], which he offered to give us a \c[4]free sample\c[0] of once they're perfected. Said ingredients can be acquired by \c[4]fighting enemies\c[0] or \c[4]buying them in shops\c[0], just like how we got everything to repair the tavern before. We should get right to it, I'm excited to try out Jeremy's new drinks!

\c[4]We gave the ingredients to Jeremy\c[0]! He put the finishing touches on his \ii[60] and \ii[61], gave us a \c[4]free sample of each\c[0], thanked us for our help, and... \c[4]immediately gave us another quest\c[0].\n

\n\c[0]Wow, I am excited to take a sip of these drinks...

quests.Title: \i[270]The Dandy Toads of Adon
Description: Seeing \c[4]Big Mouth\c[0] and \c[4]Smart Mouth\c[0] again reminded \N[4] of \c[4]Four-Toed Kevin\c[0]. Maybe it's about time we paid him a visit?

\c[4]Four-Toed Kevin\c[0]'s fur shipment was stolen! We figured this most likely has something to do with \c[4]Big Mouth, Smart Mouth, and the Dandy Toads\c[0]. While Kevin searches for more info, \c[4]we need to help Adonians affected by Dandy Toads\c[0].\n

\n\c[0]What could the Dandy Toads be doing? I still find it interesting how similar the name \"Dandy Toads\" is to \"Banditos\".

\c[4]Potential Dandy Toad activity\c[0] has been reported in the \c[4]Adonian Archipelago\c[0]. We need to get down there and fight the \c[4]Dandy Toads\c[0], then pick up \c[4]Kevin's fur shipment\c[0]!

We defeated the \c[4]Dandy Toads\c[0]! Time to return Kevin's \c[4]fur shipment\c[0]!\n

\n\c[0]I wonder when the other stolen shipments will be retrieved... eh, someone will take care of them.

We returned \c[4]Kevin's fur shipment\c[0]. He was very grateful for our help, and even gave us an \c[4]\ia[43]\c[0] as thanks! He told us that \c[4]if he ever needs help later on, he'll make sure we know\c[0].

\c[0]Always feels good to help out a friend, huh? \N[4] was right, Kevin really is a nice guy when he's not terrorizing the people of Adon.

\c[0]That \c[4]Dandy Toad Mage\c[0]... now that I'm writing this down, Kevin's fear is more understandable. Thieves wielding daggers is one thing, but if they can now harness magic, \c[4]who knows what they might try to do next\c[0]?
Objectives List: Help out someone \c[4]on the open road\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[263]A Roadside Heist
Description: A \c[4]peasant woman\c[0] reports that she was \c[4]robbed by Dandy Toads\c[0]! They ran off straight to the \c[4]Bandito Hideout\c[0].
\c[4]We defeated the Dandy Toads\c[0] and got the \ii[62]! Time to return it.
\c[4]We returned the \ii[62] to the peasant woman\c[0], and we got the reward we were promised.

quests.Title: \i[275]Negotiation By Force
Description: A \c[4]soldier's long time friend\c[0] was captured by \c[4]Dandy Toads\c[0]! They went to some \c[4]campsite\c[0], probably \N[3]'s old spot.
\c[4]We defeated the Dandy Toads\c[0] and rescued the \c[4]soldier's friend\c[0]! We should head back to the \c[4]soldier\c[0] to claim our reward.
We checked to make sure that the \c[4]soldier and his friend\c[0] were reunited, and we received our promised reward.
Objectives List: Check on the \c[4]soldier and his friend\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[276]So We Back In The Mine
Description: The objective is simple: find \ii[100] in the \c[4]Abandoned North Mine\c[0] and return them to \c[4]Will\c[0]. Nothing too crazy, for once... right?

\c[4]We returned \ii[100] to Will\c[0]. He thanked us and gave us permission to use the \c[4]magic forge\c[0] for \c[4]crafting\c[0]!\n

\n\c[0]I'm excited to see what we can make now! We will probably have to go back to that mine and get some more ores later on, though. And we can't forget about that weird \c[4]oreleaf\c[0] that regrows over time... that's definitely going to be useful.

quests.Title: \i[280]Eternal Unrest
Description:
It's our duty to \c[4]save the souls of the soldiers trapped in the Ruined Fort\c[0]. However, without the \ii[99], there's not much we can do. We need to head to the \c[4]top of the Great Pyramid\c[0] in the \c[4]Blazing Sands\c[0] to get our hands on it.\n

\n\c[0]This is some crazy stuff... and I thought trying to stop \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0] was already pretty crazy. Mixed up in that long history is this smaller yet significant one... that's weird to think about. I'm willing to bet this isn't the only time we'll find obscure yet rich history as we head down The Trail.

We obtained the \ii[99]. With it, alongside some \c[4]new equipment\c[0] we got from \c[4]The Warden\c[0], we have all the tools we need to \c[4]save the souls inside the Ruined Fort\c[0]. This is intense... but I guess we're going to get straight to it. No point in waiting!

With the \c[4]Vessel of the Forgotten General\c[0] dead, the \c[4]trapped souls\c[0] of the \c[4]Ruined Fort\c[0] were free to cross from the \c[4]Phantasmal Plane\c[0] to \c[4]the light at the end of the tunnel\c[0]. \c[4]Will\c[0] congratulated us and sent us on our way to save more of the Overworld.\n

\n\c[0]This really was a crazy journey, but even then, it's nothing compared to our quest to defeat \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0]. Although... where could that \c[4]sword\c[0] that Will was talking about be, though? I'm sure that finding it will be another crazy journey in itself.
Objectives List: Find the \ii[99] at the \c[4]top of the Great Pyramid\c[0]
Defeat the \c[4]scourge of the Ruined Fort\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[153]Gangster's Paradise
Description:
The \c[4]Crushers\c[0] (dumb name...) have been intimidating the people of \c[4]Bladesville\c[0] for some time now, apparently. The \c[4]Town Master\c[0] instructed us to go to their \c[4]base\c[0] at the \c[4]northwest of the oasis\c[0] and \c[4]show them who's boss\c[0].\n

\n\c[0]This feels like the whole mess with the Banditos all over again... yet remarkably less threatening.

After Henry slipped and told us that \c[4]the Crushers are receiving money from a mysterious source\c[0], the \c[4]Town Master\c[0] instructed us to \c[4]help out the Crushers\c[0] instead of fighting against them. \c[4]Henry\c[0] said he'll try to set up a meeting with \c[4]the mysterious guy\c[0].\n

\n\c[0]This is already many layers thicker than the Bandito ordeal... and here I thought we just had to deal with some gang of idiots and be back on our merry way.

\c[4]Henry\c[0] has mysteriously gone missing? That's strange. Our best lead right now is that he is at \c[4]a meeting with the mysterious organzation\c[0] somewhere in \c[4]Haven Harbor\c[0]... probably.\n

\n\c[4]Sam Payne\c[0]... he helped us a lot with Laeryidyean. I'm sure it wouldn't be too much of a stretch to bet that \c[4]he knows something about all this\c[4], right?

\c[4]Henry\c[0] is somewhere in the \c[4]Haven Harbor Underground\c[0], according to \c[4]Sam Payne\c[0]. We need to enter it via \c[4]the rightmost door in Sam's basement\c[0] and go find \c[4]Henry\c[0].\n

\n\c[0]How did we never find out about this before? I did always wonder what that steel door under Sam's shop was.

It would appear that a mysterious group known as the \c[4]Catalysts\c[0] have orchestrated some massive scheme. Their leader, \c[4]Terry\c[0], has been focusing the group's efforts on \c[4]designing and creating some sort of super-powerful handheld weapon\c[0]. There also appears to be plans to... \c[4]drain the oasis and mine underneath it to find some rare ore\c[0]?

\c[0]When \N[4] told me about this, it all seemed a bit ridiculous, but when she actually showed us the plans, it started to dawn on us that this was really their plan... whatever their plan is. A lot of questions are still up in the air; the \c[4]Town Master\c[0] will contact us once more after he mulls over the recent events.

\c[0]Wow... this is a lot to think about. I'm going to need to take a few nights just to process everything that just happened.
Objectives List: Raid the \c[4]Crusher base\c[0] at the \c[4]northwest of the oasis\c[0]
Steal the \ii[79] from the \c[4]Sand Stomper Sanctum\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[520]Certified Rare Enemy!
Description:
A nice woman told us that apparently there are some \c[4]rare variants of enemies\c[0] running around in \c[4]certain dungeons\c[0]. They \c[4]very rarely will appear when fighting the normal variant of their respective enemy\c[0].\n

\n\c[0]A good place to find a rare enemy would probably be the \c[4]Dark Forest\c[0]. There are both Blue Slimes and Giant Rats there, giving us a better chance of finding a rare enemy.

We got the \ii[122]\c[0] and showed it to the woman! Finding a rare enemy was really hard, but the rewards were definitely worth it. I'd like to look for more, but \N[4] got pretty impatient last time...

quests.Title: \i[116]Prophecy Defiers
Description:
The \c[4]king\c[0] has sent us to \c[4]get intel on Apocalypse and the Overworld's history\c[0]. He's apparently heard of \c[4]some of our old friends\c[0], albeit not by name, so I guess we just have to \c[4]seek them out and interrogate them about... something\c[0].\n

\n\c[0]I'll be making another journal entry after speaking to everyone.

Well. That was certainly an experience. I'm now... kind of scared, to be honest. I know I'm not supposed to, as the leader of the party and the potential chosen knight, but... I'm still me, and this is bigger than me. But I have to put that fear aside. We're probably fighting \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0] soon, and I can't afford to hesitate on anything. Well, \c[4]here's what we learned from our old friends\c[0]...

\c[4]Andy\c[0]: Seems like \c[4]those stories \N[3] heard about the exiled Immortal were true\c[0]. And if \c[30]his soul\c[0] has really escaped and been wreaking havoc on the Overworld... \c[4]could that \c[30]soul\c[4] be behind \c[18]Apocalypse\c[4]'s power, somehow\c[0]?

\c[4]Clara\c[0]: Now this one was just crazy... \c[18]Apocalypse\c[4] and Laeryidyean, brothers, and the champions from 1000 years ago\c[0]? Although... now that I know \c[4]that was \c[18]Apocalypse\c[4] in my dream after defeating Laeryidyean\c[0], this kind of makes sense. And they're both \c[4]swordmages, just like the two brothers chosen as champions 1000 years ago\c[0]... seems scarily possible.

\c[4]Sam Payne\c[0]: I should have realized this from \c[4]Laeryidyean's ramblings\c[0], but yeah, \c[4]all this chaos has been happening for a long time\c[0]. Either Laeryidyean was tens of thousands of years old... \c[4]or he was possessed by \c[30]something\c[0] that's been around that long\c[0].

\c[4]Will\c[0]: Will's information was kind of scattered, but when you put it together, it makes some sense. \c[4]The Overworld is really, really, old, and with all the history being lost about ancient man-made structures, it's clear that humanity has been destroyed at least several times\c[0]. It makes sense, there's no way just the Great Collapse could have wiped so much information off the world.

\c[4]Mark\c[0]: Mark didn't really know anything about all this right away, but he did point out that \c[4]monster activity has had a huge influx in the past decade or so\c[0]. I also believe in his expectation that \c[4]they are preparing for something\c[0]. And that something could be \c[4]another Great Collapse\c[0]... I very much hope that's not the case. But the \c[4]1000 year mark is coming up\c[0]...

\c[0]Just writing this information gives me chills down my spine. But I suppose I should just take this information and try to use it to prevent whatever went wrong 1000 years ago... or, I guess, during any time humanity has been destroyed. Because it's clear now, \c[4]we are in some sort of evil cycle caused by that \c[30]evil god's soul\c[0].
Objectives List: Speak with \c[4]two sweethearts helping the new generation of adventurers\c[0]
Speak with \c[4]a mysterious magician in a port town\c[0]
Speak with \c[4]the keeper of the magic forge\c[0]
Speak with \c[4]Adon's Hermit of the Woodlands\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[1202]The Hero's Blade
quests.Location: Telluria Castle - Chamber of the Seal
Description: So... \c[4]the First Knight's sword might still be out there somewhere\c[0]. It must have been \c[4]over a hundred thousand years since he wielded it\c[0], no? But... \c[4]I suppose any advantage over \c[18]Apocalypse\c[4] is worth looking into\c[0].
Objectives List: Learn more about the \c[4]First Knight's sword\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[780]Kitchen Nightmares
Description:
A \c[4]fancy chef\c[0] in the Market District needs our help \c[4]gathering ingredients for his soup\c[0].\n

\n\c[0]He says his \c[4]secret kitchen\c[0] is somewhere in the \c[4]Lost Forest\c[0].

That encounter was ridiculous... I did not expect us to face off against an \c[4]evil clown\c[0], let alone be \c[4]thrown inside the fancy chef's soup and have to fight our way out\c[0]. On that note, what was wrong with that soup? It was almost like we were inside some sort of creature... gross. Now I don't really want to eat it. This is the last time I'm going to help out a chef... unless it's Larry. Larry is pretty cool.
Objectives List: Collect the \ii[257]\c[0] on the ground in the \c[4]Frozen Labyrinth\c[0]
Collect the \ii[258] by fishing at \c[4]Haven Harbor's dock\c[0]
Locate the \c[4]Fancy Kitchen\c[0] in the \c[4]Lost Forest\c[0]
Present the ingredients to the \c[4]fancy chef\c[0]
Fight your way out of the \c[4]Fancy Stew\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[122]Sit Back & Relax
Description: Construction starts again! This time around, \c[4]Jeremy\c[0] wants us to get materials for chairs and tables. I'm sure that more seating will attract a lot more people to the tavern.
\c[4]Jeremy got the materials\c[0], and \c[4]construction on new seating has begun\c[0]! Like last time, he wants us to help out with \c[4]another potion ingredient hunt\c[0].

quests.Title: \i[578]It's A Healthy Habit
Description: \c[4]Jeremy\c[0]'s back to \c[4]potion brewing\c[0]. We're back to hunting for materials in dangerous places... but hey, anything for some \c[4]free samples\c[0], right?

\c[4]Jeremy's demands were satisfied\c[0]! The materials we got provided the final touches to the \ii[162] and \ii[39]. We got \c[4]free samples of each\c[0], I'm excited to try them! Oh, and \c[4]Jeremy\c[0]'s tasked us with \c[4]yet more material gathering\c[0].

quests.Title: \i[112]Keeping In Touch
Description: The Overworld's connection with Trailia still exists, but it's very thin and continues to grow weaker. To \c[4]strengthen the remaining connections\c[0], I, with the help of my friends, have to \c[4]find and interact with every Trailia statue\c[0]. According to the \c[4]Village Headman\c[0], momentary contact with Trailia should be all I need to perform maintainence. The best part? We get \c[4]a permanent blessing for each statue\c[0]!

quests.Title: \i[532]Riding The Storm
Description: \c[4]Oriel\c[0]'s spell revealed that something is amiss in Greenfield! This is probably \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0]'s doing... what could he be up to?

When we reached \c[4]Greenfield\c[0], we were horrified to find the town in \c[4]ruins\c[0]... \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0] really is a monster! He had no problem killing all those innocent people... but not all hope is lost. We found a group of \c[4]survivors\c[0] in the \c[4]ruins of the mayor's office\c[0], and they're working on a way out. Even though we couldn't save most of Greenfield, we got here just in time to help the few people remaining.

We successfully \c[4]saved Greenfield's survivors\c[0]! It was a tough journey through that tunnel, but we finally came out the other end in \c[4]Solus Valley\c[0]. The \c[4]Village Chief\c[0] generously offered to provide shelter for the survivors.\n

\n\c[0]It looks like the \c[4]Village Chief\c[0] has more for us, though. There's \"\c[4]a special guest\c[0]\" waiting for us in the \c[4]Solus Valley Jail\c[0] that could give us some leads...

We tried to interrogate \c[4]the weird guy\c[0], but we were interrupted by his fellow cult members. After a heated battle, we ran back to the guy... \c[4]only to find that they murdered him to keep him quiet\c[0].

\c[0]This Order of the \c[30]Purple\c[0]... they really aren't messing around. If everything that guy said about \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0] is true, maybe they'll have a lead on \c[18]him\c[0]? We're grasping for straws at this point, but it's all we have.

\c[0]Our next task is to return to \c[4]Telluria Castle Town\c[0] and locate the \c[4]Castle Town Sewers\c[0]. There should be a \c[4]well\c[0] somewhere that'll give us easy access to the sewers.

We successfully defeated the \c[4]Sentinel\c[0]! It was a tough fight, especially since we weren't expecting it, but we prevailed!\n

\n\c[0]They keep mentioning \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0]... maybe the Order of the \c[30]Purple\c[0] is more involved than we thought? I was starting to think this was a dead end, but they might lead us straight to \c[18]him\c[0].

From saving the survivors of \c[4]Greenfield\c[0] to infiltrating the \c[4]Order of the \c[30]Purple\c[0], we've done a lot since \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0] escaped. After all that, we finally have a solid lead on \c[18]him\c[0].

\N[3] figured out that the \c[4]Order of the \c[30]Purple\c[0] plans to \c[4]resurrect\c[0] the \c[4]corpse of the fallen god\c[0]! Their ritual of possessing the statue with a fragment of the \c[30]Purple\c[0] was just practice for the real thing. The corpse is in the \c[4]$WASTELAND\c[0], a barren region in the \c[4]southwestern part of Telluria\c[0].

\c[0]What threats lie ahead? The last few fights have pretty intense... I can't imagine what \c[18]Apocalypse\c[0] has in store for us.
Objectives List: Find your way through the \c[4]Greenfield-Solus Passage\c[0]
Defend \c[4]Solus Valley\c[0] against the \c[4]Order of the \c[30]Purple\c[0]
Locate a \c[4]well\c[0] in \c[4]Telluria Castle Town\c[0] that leads to the \c[4]Castle Town Sewers\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[633]Mine Over Matter
Description: After \c[4]Donovan upgraded our pickaxe\c[0] into a \ii[184], he instructed us to make our way to the \c[4]Abandoned North Mine\c[0] in \c[4]Bladesville\c[0] and go mining. A new \c[4]second floor\c[0] will become available to us, and we'll be able to craft \c[4]powerful equipment using tellurium\c[0]!
We succesfully forged a \ii[176] and showed it to \c[4]Donovan\c[0]. He finally showed a bit of emotion when he was pleased with our work. Now, time to make \c[4]some super strong equipment\c[0]!
Objectives List: Craft a \ii[176] and show it to \c[4]Donovan\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[603]Gone With the Wind
Description:
We were informed of a nuisance that's been troubling patrols around the Lost Forest. It's been \c[4]causing high wind speeds\c[0] and \c[4]swooping down\c[0] on unsuspecting groups of soldiers.\n

\n\c[0]This monster has evaded combat for some time, but there might be a solution. A \c[4]mysterious merchant\c[0] has set up shop in the \c[4]Blazing Sands\c[0], and they allegedly provide \c[4]a way to summon\c[0] the monster. The only catch? They solely appear \c[4]at night\c[0] in the \c[4]outskirts of the desert\c[0].
We successfully defeated the \c[4]Ethereal Bat\c[0]! We were nearly blown away during the battle, but we held our own and slayed the winged beast.
Objectives List: Purchase the \ii[228] from the \c[4]Night Merchant\c[0] in the \c[4]Blazing Sands\c[0]
Use the \ii[228] in a spot \c[4]outside the World Tree\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[1250]Telluria Field Fishing
Description: \c[4]It's time to fish!\c[0] A fisherman in Telluria Castle Town has offered to start us on the path towards a \c[4]successful fishing career\c[0]!
After catching the elusive \ii[222]\c[0], we were able to upgrade to the \ii[125]\c[0]! Now that we can catch all kinds of fish with ease, we can stock up on \c[4]powerful dishes\c[0].
Objectives List: Give the items to the \c[4]fisherman\c[0] in \c[4]The Promenade\c[0]
Give the items to the \c[4]fisherman\c[0] in \c[4]The Promenade\c[0]
Give the items to the \c[4]fisherman\c[0] in \c[4]The Promenade\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[438]Today's Training
Description:
It's time to train with the \c[4]Village Headman\c[0]! But it seems like \c[4]something is different\c[0] today...\n

\n\c[0]I was astonished when he informed me we'd be fighting a real, live monster! Will my \iw[1] and \ia[1] hold up? And what could his \c[4]motives\c[0] be...

The \c[4]Village Headman\c[0]'s motives were revealed: he intended for me to partake in \c[4]combat against a monster\c[0]!\n

\n\c[0]I've never seen something like that slimy creature before... are all monsters this ugly?
Objectives List: Speak to the \c[4]Village Headman\c[0] in the \c[4]forest\c[0]
Speak to the \c[4]Village Headman\c[0] at \c[4]his home\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[577]Back In Blood
Description: \c[4]Andy\c[0] gifted us an ancient dagger that was unearthed below Telluria Castle during an investigation. It's weakened, but if we take good of it, we might be able to \c[4]restore it to its former glory\c[0]!

quests.Title: \i[1689]The Distraught Fisherman
Description: We found a \c[4]guy freaking out\c[0] over not being able to catch a \ii[267]. In a fit of frustration, he \c[4] gave us\c[0] a \ii[249] and offered us a lot of money to \c[4]catch that fish\c[0].
We caught the \ii[267] and gave it to the \c[4]distressed sailor\c[0]. He seemed satisfied and paid us.

quests.Title: \i[1702]First Fishing Challenge
Description: A \c[4]fisherman\c[0] in \c[4]Solus Valley\c[0] challenged us to catch a \ii[274]. If we can pull it off, he'll gift us his old \ii[250], free of charge!
We caught the \ii[274] and showed it to the \c[4]fisherman\c[0]. He was impressed and held up his end of the bargain, upgrading us to his old \ii[250]!
Objectives List: Show a \ii[274] to the \c[4]fisherman\c[0] in \c[4]Solus Valley\c[0]

quests.Title: \i[291]High Protein Diet
Description: There's a \c[4]\"HELP WANTED\" sign\c[0] outside \c[4]Larry's Marvelous Diner\c[0] asking for \c[4]\"BEAR HUNTERS OF ANY SKILL LEVEL\"\c[0]... I should go ask \c[4]Larry\c[0] what's up with that.

When I asked \c[4]Larry\c[0] about the \c[4]"HELP WANTED sign\c[0], he told me he wasn't offering a job, but rather a \c[4]bounty\c[0]... on \c[4]bears\c[0].

\c[0]In desperate need of \ii[107], \c[4]Larry\c[0] has offered to pay us \i[262]150B for just \c[4]five\c[0] of 'em. What a steal!

\c[0]Shouldn't be too hard... according to \c[4]Larry\c[0], there are \c[4]Brown Bears\c[0] scattered throughout \c[4]Adon\c[0].
\c[4]Larry\c[0]'s request is fulfilled! We gave him \c[4]five\c[0] \ii[107]\c[0], and he gave us \i[262]150B as promised... along with a \ia[244]\c[0]? I can't imagine where he got this from...

quests.Title: \i[2114]Ssssssecret Family Recipe!
Description:
\c[4]Dalia\c[0], owner of \c[4]Dalia's Bear 'n Grille\c[0] in \c[4]Bladesville\c[0], told us about a \c[4]old family recipe\c[0] she's trying to revive. However, she's low on ingredients, and requires \c[4]five\c[0] \ii[291]\c[0], \c[4]three\c[0] \ii[290]\c[0], and a \ii[120]\c[0].\n

\nApparently, the final result is some delicious pie... I wonder how it'll taste?

We fulfilled \c[4]Dalia\c[0]'s request! Using the ingredients we gave her, she baked a \c[4]delicious pie\c[0] unlike anything I've tasted before! She gave us \c[4]a complimentary sample\c[0], and let us in on the secret \c[4]recipe\c[0] as promised.
